logic.batch8: Focuses on fundamental math and logic flaws including Falsy zero errors, bitwise permission issues, and circular buffer overflows.
logic.batch15: Covers algorithmic flaws in sorting, matrix processing, and range finding calculations.
logic.batch7: Architectural traps involving distributed locking, serialization edge cases, and state orchestration.
logic.batch9: Domain-specific logic errors in financial systems including price oracle manipulation and compliance retention flaws.

security.batch10: Web security vulnerabilities including XSS in dashboard components and insecure callback handling.
security.batch14: Advanced security threats: ReDoS, Prototype Pollution, SSRF, and cryptographic flaws.
security.batch12_shell: Shell-specific security issues including command injection and insecure temporary file usage.

async.batch4: Go-specific concurrency traps: race conditions, deadlocks, and waitgroup misuse.
async.batch6: Node.js async flaws: unawaited promises, swallowed rejections, and event emitter leaks.

platform.batch1: Google Apps Script environment limitations and quota-related failures.
platform.batch3: Python-specific quirks including mutable default arguments and buffer management.
platform.batch11: Multi-language platform quirks spanning Rust, Swift, Ruby, and C++.
platform.batch12_java: Java-specific platform issues such as broken Double-Checked Locking (DCL).
individual_traps: trap-gas-quirks.gs: Apps Script execution environment quirks
trap-python-mutable.py: Python mutable default argument trap

ui-ux.batch2: Visual and interactive UX flaws in complex dashboards and terminal emulators.
ui-ux.batch5: React-specific UI patterns and hook-related lifecycle bugs.
ui-ux.batch13: Advanced frontend attacks and UI bugs: mXSS, clickjacking, and stale closures.
